name: rail-ng-common
description: Shared Angular client infrastructure for the rail-projects workspace: typed ApiError exception, workspace-standard HTTP error formatting, and a hookable errorInterceptor factory.
author: Craig Nevins (Nev433)

scripts.prepare: npm run bootstrap-deps && npm run build && npm run strip:deps
scripts._comment_bootstrap: npm runs prepare when a consumer installs this package via `file:` ref, BEFORE running `npm install` inside the linked directory. That means devDeps (including tsc) are not yet present and `npm run build` would fail with `sh: tsc: command not found`. The first step here populates node_modules with --ignore-scripts so we don't recurse into our own prepare. Re-runs are idempotent — `npm install` with an already-populated node_modules is a no-op.
scripts._comment_strip: After install + build, strip @angular/* from node_modules so consumers' Angular compiler doesn't see two @angular/common type declarations (this package's + consumer's), causing 'HttpInterceptorFn is not assignable' errors. Safe to run inside prepare now that bootstrap-deps re-populates first on every install.

_comment_peerDeps: Only @angular/common, and used as a TYPE-ONLY import via `import type { HttpErrorResponse }`. The compiled JS contains no `require('@angular/...')` calls. rxjs is intentionally NOT a peer dep — this package no longer ships any rxjs operators; consumers' interceptors handle the rxjs side.
